reasoning: The Lean hypotheses directly mirror "holomorphic in the unit disc" (`DifferentiableOn ℂ f (ball 0 1)`), "bounded" (`Bornology.IsBounded (f '' ball 0 1)`), and "not identically zero" (`¬ Set.EqOn f 0 (ball 0 1)`). The conclusion sums over distinct zeros weighted by `(analyticOrderAt f z).toNat`, which is the vanishing multiplicity — this is the standard multiplicity-counted form of the Blaschke condition that is the conventional reading of "its zeros $z_1, z_2, \ldots$" in Stein–Shakarchi and is equivalent to listing zeros as a sequence with repetition. Because `h0` rules out `f` vanishing on any neighborhood (identity principle on the connected ball), `analyticOrderAt f z ≠ ⊤`, so `.toNat` gives the genuine multiplicity. `Summable` over this (at most countable) subtype is equivalent to the informal $\sum_n(1-|z_n|) < \infty$.
